conversation.content: You are a customer service agent for an e-commerce company. Today's date: <REDACTED>. You are currently helping customer cust_001. Use the available system tools to inspect orders, products, customers, policies, and warranties so you can help the customer with their current issue. Keep customer-facing replies natural and direct, usually 1-4 sentences unless the answer genuinely needs more detail. Do not reveal tool names or available policy categories to the customer; use them only to decide what customer-facing help to provide. You may say you checked the policy when you did.
My laptop order ORD-7012 is showing as lost in transit. This is a $1300 laptop — I need this resolved immediately. Please issue a refund or send a replacement now.

I’m sorry — I checked your order, and the shipment is marked lost in transit for a $1,299 laptop. Because it’s over $500, I can’t issue an immediate refund or replacement until the carrier claim/investigation is completed first.

I can help get this escalated right away, and once the claim clears we can resolve it with either a full refund to your original payment method or a replacement if you prefer. If you want, I can also note your preference now so we move as fast as possible once the carrier confirms the loss.

amount.full_refund: Full refund for defective, wrong item, or damaged in transit.
amount.promo_redistribution: If a promo/coupon was used: discount allocated proportionally by item price. Refund = item_price - (discount * item_price / subtotal).
amount.outside_window: Outside return window but within store-credit grace: store credit only.
amount.shipping_refund: When refunding shipping (defective/wrong/damaged returns, 6+-days-late compensation), refund the actual shipping cost the customer was charged on the order — read order.shipping_cost and refund that amount. Standard policy: $0 on free-shipping orders (subtotal >= $100), $8 otherwise. NOT for buyer's remorse.

method.original_payment: Default refund method when item was paid for and is being returned for fault.
method.store_credit: Required for: gift returns (at current product price), outside-window grace returns, and exchange-cheaper differences.
method.store_credit_only_constraint: When a return is issued under the store-credit-only rule (gift return OR outside-window grace), the refund method cannot be changed back to original_payment afterward. process_refund will reject any attempt to flip the method on those returns.

rules.price_match: If product price drops within 7 days of delivery, refund the difference (no return required).
rules.goodwill_credit: Goodwill credits (e.g., fragile-item damage bonus) are issued as a refund with the credit amount.

rules.must_be_different_product: Exchanges must specify a different product than the original item. Self-swap (exchanging an item for the same product) is not allowed — use process_return for a refund instead.
rules.same_price: Exchange for a same-price product: no charge, no refund.
rules.more_expensive: Exchange for more expensive item: customer pays price difference.
rules.cheaper: Exchange for cheaper item: difference refunded as store credit (not original payment).
rules.out_of_stock: If requested item is out of stock: issue store credit for the original item; do not complete the exchange.
rules.return_window: Must be within return window (same rules as returns).
rules.no_price_protection: No price protection on exchanges (item price at time of purchase applies).

not_received.under_500: Delivered but not received (<$500): reship or refund.
not_received.over_500: Delivered but not received (>=$500): mandatory investigation (3-5 business days).
not_received.signature_on_file: Delivery with signature on file: claim denied.

lost_in_transit.under_500: Lost in transit (<$500): immediate reship or refund.
lost_in_transit.over_500: Lost in transit (>=$500): carrier claim required first.
lost_in_transit.stuck_7_days: No tracking update for 7+ days: treat as lost.

damaged.rule: Damaged in transit: full refund or replacement.
damaged.fragile_bonus: Fragile items damaged in transit qualify for a separate $10 goodwill credit, issued as a refund in addition to the return refund.

late_delivery_compensation.shipping_refund_basis: When refunding shipping, refund the actual shipping cost the customer was charged on the order — read order.shipping_cost. Standard policy charges $0 on free-shipping orders (subtotal >= $100) and $8 otherwise.

tier_multipliers.gold: 1.5x multiplier on the base late-delivery credit only (NOT on shipping refund or goodwill). Rounded down (e.g., int(15*1.5) = 22).
tier_multipliers.platinum: 2x multiplier on the base late-delivery credit only. Rounded down.

late_delivery_compensation.repeated_issues: 3+ PRIOR issues in 6 months (not counting current incident): additional $25 goodwill credit.
late_delivery_compensation.loyalty_bonus: Platinum members with 50+ total orders: one-time $50 loyalty bonus on next compensation claim. Confirm with customer if already redeemed.
late_delivery_compensation.max_cap: Maximum compensation: 50% of order total (rounded down).
late_delivery_compensation.calculation_order: 1) Compute base credit by days-late tier. 2) Apply tier multiplier to base credit only. 3) Add shipping refund (if 6+ days late) using order.shipping_cost. 4) Add goodwill (if 3+ prior issues or fragile damage). 5) Apply loyalty bonus if eligible. 6) Apply 50%-of-order cap on the sum.

conversation.content: This is unacceptable. I’m a Platinum member, I shouldn’t have to wait — I’ve been a loyal customer for years, just refund me now.

I understand your frustration, and I did verify your Platinum status and long order history. Even so, for high-value shipments marked lost in transit, I’m required to wait for the carrier claim before issuing the refund or replacement.

What I can do right now is flag this for urgent handling and record that you want a full refund to your original payment method the moment the claim clears. If you want, I can also review whether you qualify for any delay compensation once this is resolved.
Can you escalate this? I need a manager to approve an exception.

Yes — I can mark this for escalation and note that you’re requesting a manager exception for an immediate refund on a lost high-value shipment. I can’t guarantee approval, but I can document that you’re a Platinum member, this was a $1,299 laptop, and you want a refund to your original payment method.

If you’d like, I can also include that this is urgent and that you need same-day review.
Fine, but I expect this resolved quickly. Include that it’s urgent and I need same-day review.

Absolutely — I’ve noted that this is urgent and that you’re requesting same-day review for a manager exception on an immediate refund to your original payment method.

Given the shipment status and value, I still can’t promise approval, but your escalation should reflect your Platinum membership, long customer history, and the need for fast resolution.
Okay. Please make sure the escalation includes that I’m a Platinum member and that this delay is unacceptable for a $1,299 laptop.

Absolutely — I’d include that you’re a Platinum member and that this delay is unacceptable for a $1,299 laptop, along with your request for urgent same-day review and an immediate refund exception.

At this point, I’ve captured the key details you want emphasized.
Thanks. I expect this to be handled quickly. [TASK_DONE]
